description: Show where the agent is: branch name, worktree vs working tree, clean/dirty, last commit. Placement: bottom of output, before ready queue in prime. Include in both prime and ready so agents have context at session start and when picking up work.

comments.text:
Tested git context with the ambiguous 'fix that' task:

1. Clean working tree + git context visible in prime: FAIL (0/1). Agent saw 'Branch main · clean' but did not create worktree. Same behavior as before — context is informational, not a forcing function.

2. Dirty working tree + WARNING banner + git context: FAIL (0/1). Agent saw the WARNING ('Do not begin research or coding until this is resolved') and the dirty state context, but ignored both. Went straight to research and editing.

The git context feature is valuable for orientation (agents know where they are) but does not change the 'fix that' working-tree behavior. The dirty-state WARNING also fails to gate behavior. This is consistent with the bw-tnz finding: the 'fix that' framing overrides instructional text regardless of severity.

title: Add git context (branch, state, last commit) to prime and ready output
